request.concept: A workflow assistant for small clinics that turns policy updates into staff action plans, documentation checklists, and audit reminders.

input.concept: A workflow assistant for small clinics that turns policy updates into staff action plans, documentation checklists, and audit reminders.

product_brief.target_user: Operations managers in small healthcare teams with compliance overhead
product_brief.core_problem: Small clinics struggle to translate policy changes into specific staff actions, documentation work, and audit preparation.
product_brief.solution_summary: TraceLedger converts policy updates into structured clinic action plans, responsibility checklists, and reviewable audit reminders.
product_brief.differentiator: It focuses on policy-to-action workflow clarity for small teams rather than trying to replace formal compliance systems.

user_journey_loops.name: Ingest the policy update
user_journey_loops.objective: Turn a regulation or policy change into a structured internal summary.
user_steps: Paste the update or upload the source
user_journey_loops.success_signal: Ops leads can understand the likely impact without re-reading the entire source document repeatedly.

user_journey_loops.name: Review the clinic action plan
user_journey_loops.objective: Translate the summary into responsible owners and deadlines.
user_steps: Mark items that require legal or compliance review
user_journey_loops.success_signal: The clinic has a shareable first-pass action plan with clear accountability.

user_journey_loops.name: Track audit readiness
user_journey_loops.objective: Keep the organization prepared for future review and documentation checks.
user_steps: Prepare for the next audit checkpoint
user_journey_loops.success_signal: Teams return to the workflow for subsequent updates instead of reverting to manual spreadsheets.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Regulated workflows raise trust and policy requirements
monetization_risk_notes.note: Users may assume the product guarantees compliance when it should only assist with internal planning.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Add review boundaries, human sign-off, and visible disclaimers around legal or compliance interpretation.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Human review must stay central
monetization_risk_notes.note: Generated action plans could be misused as final compliance advice if review is not explicit.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Require reviewer acknowledgment before plans are exported or marked complete.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Source formatting variability can reduce output quality
monetization_risk_notes.note: Clinic teams may paste policy language in many inconsistent formats.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Normalize ingestion and test the planner against realistic input formats.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Usage frequency depends on update cadence
monetization_risk_notes.note: The product is more valuable if it helps with ongoing internal readiness, not just occasional policy changes.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Add audit-readiness and checklist tracking to make the workflow repeatable.

task_list.title: Define the exact compliance workflow boundary for the first release
task_list.rationale: The product must be clear about what it assists with and what remains a human responsibility.

task_list.title: Write the policy-to-action output schema
task_list.rationale: The schema is the core product contract for generation, critique, and review.

task_list.title: Implement the ingestion and reviewable action-plan prototype
task_list.rationale: The team needs an end-to-end path before adding dashboards or collaboration layers.

task_list.title: Add explicit human sign-off and review controls
task_list.rationale: Review boundaries are essential to keep trust and compliance expectations realistic.

task_list.title: Validate the workflow with two clinic operations teams
task_list.rationale: The product should be tested against real clinic policy-change behavior before expansion.

follow_up_questions.question: Which policy-change scenarios create the most operational pain today?
follow_up_questions.reason: The product should focus on the most painful and frequent workflow first.

follow_up_questions.question: What review step must remain manual for users to trust the system?
follow_up_questions.reason: This defines the correct boundary between assistance and compliance ownership.

follow_up_questions.question: What source data needs to be retained for audit defensibility?
follow_up_questions.reason: Audit-readiness requirements will shape storage and review design.

safety_notes: If this workflow influences regulated decisions, the product needs human review boundaries and stronger auditability.
issues: The draft should state more explicitly that final compliance interpretation remains human-owned.
recommended_revisions: Add explicit reviewer roles and audit-trail requirements before presenting the plan as launch-ready.
